title: Fix indefinite 'connecting' state blocking app entry

requirements.text: Investigate and fix the indefinite 'connecting' state that prevents users from entering the app. Identify where the connecting screen/state is triggered (likely in authentication flow, Internet Identity initialization, or actor setup) and ensure it transitions correctly to the authenticated state or displays a meaningful error with a retry option if the connection genuinely fails.

quotes: It keeps saying connecting i can't get in

acceptanceCriteria: Users are no longer stuck indefinitely on a 'connecting' screen
After Internet Identity login, the app proceeds to the lobby or room page without hanging
If connection fails after a timeout, a clear error message is shown with a retry button
The connecting state resolves within a reasonable time (e.g., under 10 seconds on normal network)

requirements.text: Add a timeout mechanism to the authentication/actor initialization flow so that if the 'connecting' state does not resolve within 15 seconds, the app exits the connecting state, displays a user-friendly error message, and offers a retry or logout option.

quotes: It keeps saying connecting i can't get in

acceptanceCriteria: A timeout is enforced on the connecting/initialization phase
After timeout, the user sees an error message (not a blank or frozen screen)
A 'Try Again' or 'Return to Login' button is available after timeout

constraints: Do not modify files in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts directly — use wrappers or fix consuming components instead
nonGoals: Changes to WebRTC signaling or video feed logic
Backend schema or data model changes
New UI features unrelated to the connection/auth flow
